trails.name: Rotary Centennial Loop Track
trails.description: Walk a loop track through native bush.

trails.name: Wetland Track
trails.description: Walk the Wetland Track and explore WWII sites.

trails.name: Te Araroa Track, Govan Wilson Rd To Dome
LANGSVIEW TRACK
trails.description: For amazing views of the countryside and coastline, follow this track along the ridgeline of the Brynderwyn range. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Dome - Viewing Platform To Waiwhu Rd Tk
Goldie Bush - Horsman Rd to Constable Rd
Dome - Tearooms To Viewing Platform Tk
Dome Forest car park to Govan Wilson Rd, Matakana
trails.description: A challenging longer walk (7 hrs) through regenerating native forest, the track starts at the Dome carpark and ends at Govan Wilson Rd, Matakana. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Dome Forest car park to lookout
trails.description: Starting from the Dome tearooms carpark, steps lead up along a narrow road reserve to the Dome Forest conservation area.

trails.name: Dome Forest car park to Waiwhiu Grove
trails.description: From the Dome Forest lookout the track continues to the Dome Summit where you get views of Taranga Island.

trails.name: Goldie Bush Walkway
trails.description: Goldie Bush Scenic Reserve offers a variety of walks through coastal kauri forest, taking in the impressive Mokoroa Falls.

trails.name: Mangawhai Cliffs Walkway
trails.description: This walk goes from the shoreline to the clifftops. You'll get to see remarkable views up and down the coast and to off-shore islands. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Maungauika/North Head Historic Walk
trails.description: Explore defences that were placed on the headland to protect Auckland from feared Russian invasions.

trails.name: Moirs Hill Walkway
trails.description: Stroll to view the Pohuehue waterfall or complete the walkway or longer loop (Beverley Price Loop Track) through regenerating forest in the Pohuehue Scenic Reserve, just south of Warkworth.

trails.name: Mokoroa Falls Track
trails.description: Walk the Mokoroa Falls Track to view the falls from a lookout platform. The track can be walked as part of a loop track when joined with the Mokoroa Stream Track and Goldie Bush Walkway.

trails.name: Mokoroa Stream Track
trails.description: The Mokoroa Stream Track is a challenging loop walk which comes off the Goldie Bush Walkway.

trails.name: Motuora Island walks
trails.description: Motuora is a small island with walks of up to two hours. Walk to the northern end of the island or to Still Bay, a perfect place for a picnic.

trails.name: Motutapu Walkway
trails.description: The Motutapu Walkway takes you from Islington Bay on Rangitoto Island across the causeway to Home Bay and Emu Bay on Motutapu Island.

trails.name: Mount Auckland Atuanui Walkway
trails.description: Take a walk to the summit of Mount Auckland (Atuanui) through regenerating native forest. At the summit you’ll have panoramic views of Kaipara Harbour and Hoteo River Estuary.

trails.name: Mt Tamahunga Walkway
trails.description: Tramp up to the summit of Mt Tamahunga through regenerating native forest. This track is part of Te Araroa - The Long Pathway.

trails.name: Okura Bush Walkway
trails.description: Take a 3-hour return walk along the Okura Bush Walkway. The walkway winds its way through the Okura River Estuary and through coastal forest to Karepiro Bay and historic Dacre Cottage.

trails.name: Rangitoto Island longer walks
trails.description: Explore Rangitoto Island Scenic Reserve on these longer walks. Walk around the coast of Rangitoto, to adjoining Motutapu Island, or continue on from the summit to Islington Bay.

trails.name: Rangitoto Island shorter walks
trails.description: Take a short walk around Rangitoto Island Scenic Reserve. Stroll through a kowahi grove or glen of kidney ferns, or walk along the coast to a gull breeding colony.

trails.name: Rangitoto Summit Track
trails.description: A steady climb through pōhutukawa forest and lava fields, with panoramic views of Auckland and the Hauraki Gulf from the summit of this volcanic island.

trails.name: Te Henga Walkway
trails.description: A short walk just south of Muriwai Beach to a cliff top viewing point with stunning views of Auckland's wild west coast.

trails.name: Goat Island Walkway
trails.description: This return walk traverses the coast part way to Cape Rodney. overlooking New Zealand's oldest and most popular marine reserve.

trails.name: Goldie Bush Walkway
trails.description:
Goldie Bush Scenic Reserve offers a variety of walks through coastal kauri forest, taking in the impressive Mokoroa Falls.

Goldie Bush Walkway can be walked from either Constable Rd or Horsman Rd.

From Constable Rd the walkway descends through the Motutara Scenic Reserve to cross Mokoroa Stream. The track then enters the Goldie Bush Scenic Reserve, climbing through coastal forest with regenerating kauri, exiting at Horseman Rd.

Time: 4 - 5 hr
Distance: 4.5 km

For a longer, more challenging loop walk, walk the Mokoroa Stream Track and return to the Mokoroa Falls Track via the Goldie Bush Walkway.

trails.name: Mangawhai Walkway
trails.description: To walk along the beach, you will need to know what the tide is doing. Depending on the tide times, you can go up the ridge first, or vice versa. Parts of this track are across private farm land and may be closed due to lambing.

trails.name: Matietie Walk
trails.description: This coastal walk takes you through the Matietie Historic Reserve.

trails.name: Moirs Hill Walkway
trails.description: The Moirs Hill Walkway and Beverley Price Loop Track are tramping tracks. They are steep in places and can be rough, slippery and muddy.

trails.name: Mokoroa Falls Track
trails.description:
Walk the Mokoroa Falls Track to view the falls from a lookout platform. The track can be walked as part of a loop track when joined with the Mokoroa Stream Track and Goldie Bush Walkway. 

The Mokoroa Falls Track starts at Horsman Road. You pass the junction with the Goldie Bush Walkway on your left after about 5 minutes. 

The track follows a gentle path along a ridge to a viewing platform where you can view the Mokoroa Falls.

From the viewing platform, steep steps lead down to the Mokoroa Stream where you can view the falls from the bottom. Take care as the steps are slippery.

It is 2.2 km (1 hr 15 min) back to the junction of the Goldie Bush Walkway and Mokoroa Stream Track, and a further 1.3 km (35 min) to Constable Rd.

Longer loop walk
The stone steps form the start of the Mokoroa Stream Track. For a longer 4-5 hour loop walk, walk the Mokoroa Stream Track and return to the Mokoroa Falls Track via the Goldie Bush Walkway.

trails.name: Mokoroa Stream Track
trails.description:
The Mokoroa Stream Track is a challenging loop walk which comes off the Goldie Bush Walkway. Longer loop walk
For a longer more challenging walk, continue on from the Mokoroa Stream Track to the Mokoroa Falls Track (1.5 km, 40 min one way).

After about 40 min, turn right onto the Goldie Bush Walkway. Walk 2.2 km (1 hr 15 min one way) back to the junction of Goldie Bush Walkway and Mokoroa Stream Track, and a further 1.3 km (35 min one way) to Constable Road. Continue straight from the junction of the Mokoroa Falls Track and Goldie Bush Walkway to exit at Horsman Rd (5 min one way).

The Mokoroa Stream Track links the Goldie Bush Walkway and Mokoroa Falls Track.

The track leaves the Goldie Bush Walkway at a junction just before the swingbridge. It follows the Mokoroa Stream, crossing it a number of times, before exiting on the true left of the stream, just before reaching the Mokoroa Falls.

The track climbs steeply to reach the Mokoroa Falls Track. A lookout marks the start of the Mokoroa Falls Track and gives good views of the falls.

trails.name: Motutapu Walkway
trails.description: The Motutapu Walkway takes you from Islington Bay on Rangitoto Island across the causeway to Home Bay and Emu Bay on Motutapu Island.

trails.name: Motutapu Wetland Track
trails.description: Walk through regenerating forest on the Rotary Centennial Walkway or walk the Wetland Track and explore WWII sites. The Wetland Track passes through WWII telegraph and observation post sites. A short detour will take you to gun emplacements. The track crosses farmland to Billy Goat Point, passing the Waikarapupu wetland. Billy Goat Point provides a great outlook to the north, and has another military site. Mullet, Station, Waikarapupu and Sandy Bays can be accessed from this track.

trails.name: Mount Auckland Atuanui Walkway
trails.description:
Take a walk to the summit of Mount Auckland (Atuanui) through regenerating native forest. At the summit you’ll have panoramic views of Kaipara Harbour and Hoteo River Estuary.
The Atuanui/Mount Auckland Scenic Reserve was transferred to Ngāti Whātua o Kaipara in 2013 as part of the iwi’s Treaty of Waitangi Settlement.

Walk to the summit of Mount Auckland (Atuanui) through regenerating native forest, with spectacular views over the nearby Kaipara Harbour and Hoteo River estuary. 

The walkway follows an airstrip and logging track along the ridge to the Atuanui forest, which has fine stands of regenerating rimu and kauri. It continues up a narrow ridge with side tracks to view mature kauri. 

A lookout at the summit (305 metres) provides panoramic views. From here you can retrace your steps or take the track westward to the Kaipara Coast Highway exit, which is a steeper descent. This end of the track exits through farm land to SH 16.

trails.name: Mt Tamahunga Walkway
trails.description:
Tramp up to the summit of Mt Tamahunga through regenerating native forest. This track is part of Te Araroa - The Long Pathway. 

From Omaha Valley Road:

Tramp up to the summit of Mt Tamahunga through regenerating native forest. This track joins the Te Araroa - The Long Pathway just before the summit.

Time: to the summit – 1 hr 30 min one way; to Rodney Road – 3 hr 30 min one way; to the top of Matakana Valley Road – 2hr 30 one way.
Distance: to the summit – 2.5 km one way; to Rodney Road – 6.5 km one way; to Matakana Valley Road – 5.2 km one way

The walkway initially climbs over farm paddocks to the bush line (please follow markers and use stiles to cross fences). Once in the bush, follow orange markers up the ridge to the summit and a helicopter pad. Views from the summit are restricted by forest, but several gaps in the track do provide views of the area.

From here you can retrace your steps or continue northwards to the Rodney Road exit. On leaving the bush, follow white markers up and down a steep route following a farm fence line until you exit onto a farm track, which joins with the end of Rodney Road. Enjoy magnificent views up and down the coast. You can also turn west, on reaching the summit ridge / Te Araroa track and pass the Metrological Service Radar Station, then descend gradually to the Matakana Valley Road exit, on the norther side of the Matakana hill. Part of this track follows a buried / boxed in power cable.

You can also continue the walk from Rodney Road, 3 km across private farmland to the end of Bathgate Rd, Pakiri. Follow the white markers, please stick to the route and use the stiles to cross fences.

trails.name: Rangitoto Island longer walks
trails.description: Explore Rangitoto Island Scenic Reserve on these longer walks. Walk around the coast of Rangitoto, to adjoining Motutapu Island, or continue on from the summit to Islington Bay.

trails.name: Te Henga Walkway
trails.description: A short walk just south of Muriwai Beach to a cliff top viewing point with stunning views of Auckland's wild west coast.

trails.name: Tiritiri Matangi Island walks
trails.description: See a variety of native birds on the walks on this pest-free island, as well as views of the surrounding islands.

trails.name: Langs Beach Scenic Reserve
trails.description: The Main Track (750m) is a gentle gradient following an old farm track to the SE boundary of the reserve.   It take you past a regenerating wetland before entering the bush. A short 250m Lower Loop track returns you via a pleasant streamside walk and a glade exiting onto the wetland before returning to the Main Track.  The Middle Loop takes you to the centre of the reserve to reconnect with the Main Track and provides with connections to the the Upper Loop.  The Upper Loop (1km) climbs up and down around the SW boundary of the reserve.  All tracks eventually take you back to the Main track for your return to Cove Road.  Glow worms can be seen at night along the Middle Loop track.
